senses.definition: Cars, especially when talking about them in general.
examples: The city is trying to reduce the number of autos on the road during rush hour.
Electric autos are becoming more popular every year.
He has always loved classic autos from the 1960s.

senses.definition: To save a file or document automatically at regular times without the user having to do anything.
examples: This writing app will autosave your work every two minutes.
Make sure the program is set to autosave so you do not lose any changes.
The system will autosave the game before each major battle.

senses.definition: A feature in software that saves your file or data automatically at set times.
examples: Turn on autosave in the options menu.
Thanks to autosave, I did not lose my report when the laptop crashed.
The game’s autosave can be disabled in advanced settings.

senses.definition: Saved automatically by a computer program at a certain time.
examples: You can restore the autosaved version of the document from this folder.
The file name shows that it is an autosaved copy.
He was relieved to find an autosaved draft of his essay.

senses.definition: Third person singular form of autosave: saves a file or document automatically at regular times.
examples: The software autosaves every five minutes by default.
If the system autosaves too often, it may slow down your computer.
This app autosaves your photos to the cloud.

senses.definition: Present participle of autosave: saving a file or document automatically at regular times.
examples: The program is autosaving in the background while you type.
Autosaving helps prevent data loss during a power cut.
She likes apps that are constantly autosaving her progress.

senses.definition: The action or process of saving files or data automatically.
examples: Autosaving is turned on by default in this program.
Frequent autosaving can reduce the risk of losing important work.
The game’s autosaving sometimes interrupts the flow of play.

senses.definition: The act of doing or saying something in an impromptu or improvised way.
examples: His speech was pure autoschediasm, created on the spot without notes.
The performance turned into an exercise in autoschediasm after the script was lost.
Her lecture drifted into autoschediasm as she moved away from her prepared slides.

senses.definition: Plural of autoschediasm: impromptu or improvised acts or remarks.
examples: The lecture was filled with charming autoschediasms that were not in the notes.
Her letters contain occasional autoschediasms that reveal her quick mind.
The comedian’s best jokes are often spontaneous autoschediasms.

senses.definition: Impromptu, improvised, or done without previous preparation.
examples: He delivered an autoschediastic speech when the keynote speaker failed to arrive.
Their meeting turned into an autoschediastic discussion of future plans.
The professor gave an autoschediastic history of the city, speaking entirely from memory.

senses.definition: Relating to or characterized by being impromptu or improvised.
examples: The play has an autoschediastical quality, as if it were made up in the moment.
His autoschediastical commentary kept the audience engaged despite the lack of slides.

senses.definition: In an impromptu or improvised way, without previous planning.
examples: She spoke autoschediastically for thirty minutes after losing her notes.
The musician played autoschediastically, responding to the mood of the room.
The panel answered questions autoschediastically rather than following a script.

senses.definition: To act, speak, or perform in an improvised or impromptu way.
examples: Forced to autoschediaze, he turned his lecture into a lively discussion.
They often autoschediaze their shows instead of following a strict script.
In emergencies, leaders sometimes must autoschediaze their responses.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of autoschediaze: acted or spoke in an improvised way.
examples: He autoschediazed the entire lecture after the projector failed.
They autoschediazed their way through the meeting without formal notes.
The ceremony was largely autoschediazed due to last‑minute changes.

senses.definition: Third person singular of autoschediaze: acts, speaks, or performs in an improvised way.
examples: She often autoschediazes when answering difficult questions.
The professor autoschediazes whenever the discussion moves off topic.
In the second half, the band simply autoschediazes.

senses.definition: Present participle of autoschediaze: acting, speaking, or performing in an improvised way.
examples: She was autoschediazing the story, adding details as she went.
Autoschediazing during the presentation helped him connect with the audience.
They spent the evening autoschediazing scenes from an imaginary play.

senses.definition: A medical instrument used for looking inside the body, especially into the ear or other small cavities.
examples: The doctor used an autoscope to examine the patient’s ear canal.
Modern clinics often rely on a digital autoscope for clearer images.
The nurse cleaned the autoscope before the next examination.

senses.definition: Relating to experiences in which a person seems to see their own body from outside.
examples: Patients sometimes report autoscopic experiences during surgery.
The study focused on autoscopic visions in people with epilepsy.
Autoscopic phenomena raise difficult questions for neuroscience.

senses.definition: Plural of autoscopic experience: events where someone seems to see their own body from an outside point of view.
examples: The article describes several autoscopies reported by climbers at high altitude.
Repeated autoscopies can be frightening for patients.
Researchers collect accounts of autoscopies to study brain function.

senses.definition: An experience in which a person has the sensation of seeing their own body from outside themselves.
examples: Autoscopy is sometimes reported during near‑death experiences.
The neurologist explained that autoscopy can be linked to certain brain lesions.
Her first episode of autoscopy occurred after a period of severe stress.

senses.definition: A tool or program that sends emails or messages automatically according to rules or a schedule.
examples: We use an autosender to reply to common customer questions.
The newsletter autosender went out of control and sent three copies.
You can set up an autosender for birthday greetings.

senses.definition: A process in which a body becomes sensitive to its own tissues or substances, often causing an immune reaction.
examples: Autosensitization can lead to various autoimmune conditions.
The researcher described autosensitization as a key step in the disease.
Repeated exposure triggered autosensitization in the test animals.

senses.definition: Made sensitive to substances from the same body, usually through immune processes.
examples: Autosensitized cells were detected in the patient’s blood sample.
The tissue became autosensitized after repeated inflammation.
Autosensitized patients may react strongly to their own proteins.

senses.definition: Blood poisoning caused by infection that originates within the body itself rather than from outside.
examples: The patient died from autosepticemia following a perforated ulcer.
Autosepticemia can develop rapidly if internal infections are not treated.
Surgical intervention was necessary to prevent autosepticemia.

senses.definition: Treatment of a patient using their own blood serum, usually after it has been modified.
examples: Autoserotherapy was attempted as an experimental treatment for the condition.
The clinic offers autoserotherapy for certain chronic illnesses.
Researchers are studying new forms of autoserotherapy.

senses.definition: Serum taken from a person’s own blood and used for medical treatment of that same person.
examples: The doctor prepared autoserum from the patient’s blood sample.
Autoserum was injected to stimulate the immune response.
The procedure relies on freshly produced autoserum.

senses.definition: A method in animal breeding, especially in poultry, where males and females can be told apart by their appearance soon after birth.
examples: The new chicken breed allows easy autosexing on the day of hatching.
Autosexing helps farmers separate male and female chicks quickly.
Geneticists developed lines of birds suitable for autosexing.

senses.definition: Describing breeds or lines of animals where males and females can be distinguished by appearance at hatching or birth.
examples: They chose an autosexing breed of chickens for the farm.
Autosexing strains reduce labor costs in hatcheries.
The autosexing variety shows different feather patterns in males and females.

senses.definition: To sign a document or form automatically, often using stored information or a digital signature.
examples: The system will autosign your tax forms once you approve them.
You can set the app to autosign standard contracts.
The document was autosigned by the company’s digital certificate.

senses.definition: The normal member of a pair of twins or conjoined organisms, as opposed to a smaller or dependent twin.
examples: In this rare case of conjoined twins, one was clearly the autosite.
The autosite carried most of the shared organs.
Surgeons planned the operation with the health of the autosite in mind.

senses.definition: Relating to or being the normal member in a pair of conjoined or twin organisms.
examples: The autositic twin showed normal development.
Autositic structures were preserved during surgery.
The report described the autositic host and the attached parasitic twin.

senses.definition: The skeleton formed by the body’s own tissues, as opposed to any external supporting structure.
examples: The autoskeleton of vertebrates is made of bone and cartilage.
Corals have both an autoskeleton and an external skeleton.
The study compared the autoskeleton across different species.

senses.definition: A motorized sled or sledge, usually used for moving people or goods over snow or ice.
examples: They crossed the frozen lake on an autosled.
The research team loaded their equipment onto the autosled.
An old autosled was parked beside the cabin.

senses.definition: Relating to any chromosome that is not a sex chromosome, or to traits carried on those chromosomes.
examples: Cystic fibrosis is an autosomal recessive disorder.
Researchers mapped the mutation to an autosomal region.
The disease shows autosomal dominant inheritance.

senses.definition: In a way that involves autosomes rather than sex chromosomes.
examples: The trait is inherited autosomally rather than through the X chromosome.
These conditions are passed on autosomally.
The mutation appears to be transmitted autosomally.

senses.definition: A disorder in which a person cannot correctly recognize parts of their own body.
examples: Autosomatognosis may occur after damage to the parietal lobe.
Patients with autosomatognosis can fail to identify their own limbs.
The neurologist diagnosed autosomatognosis following a series of tests.

senses.definition: Relating to or showing the inability to recognize parts of one’s own body.
examples: Autosomatognostic symptoms appeared after the stroke.
The patient’s autosomatognostic behavior included denying ownership of a limb.
Autosomatognostic signs were recorded in the clinical notes.

senses.definition: Any chromosome that is not a sex chromosome.
examples: Humans have 22 pairs of autosomes and one pair of sex chromosomes.
The mutation was found on chromosome 7, an autosome.
Most common genetic disorders are linked to an autosome.

senses.definition: Plural of autosome: the chromosomes that are not sex chromosomes.
examples: The 44 autosomes in humans carry most of our genetic information.
Scientists compared the autosomes of different primate species.
Mutations occurred on several autosomes.

senses.definition: Relating to seeking salvation or improvement through one’s own efforts.
examples: The philosophy has a strongly autosoteric emphasis on personal discipline.
Autosoteric beliefs stress that each person must save themselves.
He rejected autosoteric ideas in favor of divine grace.

senses.definition: The belief or doctrine that a person can achieve salvation or spiritual freedom by their own efforts.
examples: Many theologians criticize autosoterism as denying the need for divine help.
The movement was attacked for promoting autosoterism.
Autosoterism places the burden of salvation on the individual.

senses.definition: A non‑motile spore produced inside a parent cell, especially in certain algae, that develops into a new individual.
examples: Each cell produced four autospores that were later released.
The green alga reproduces by forming an autospore within the parent cell.
Under stress, the organism forms a resistant autospore.

senses.definition: Plural of autospore: non‑motile spores formed inside a parent cell that grow into new individuals.
examples: The autospores are released when the cell wall breaks.
Autospores ensure rapid reproduction under favorable conditions.
Microscopy revealed several autospores within each cell.

senses.definition: Relating to or producing autospores.
examples: Autosporic reproduction is common in some green algae.
The species shows an autosporic life cycle stage.
Autosporic cells were observed under the microscope.

senses.definition: A device or system that sprays liquid automatically, often for cleaning or protection.
examples: The car wash uses an autospray to cover the vehicle with soap.
Install an autospray to water the garden while you are away.
The lab’s autospray system disinfects equipment overnight.

senses.definition: To spray something automatically using a device.
examples: The machine will autospray detergent onto the dishes.
The system autosprays the crops at sunrise.
These units autospray disinfectant every hour.

senses.definition: Plural of autostability: conditions in which a system keeps itself stable without outside control.
examples: Different autostabilities were observed under changing loads.
Engineers analyzed the autostabilities of the control system.
Biological systems show various autostabilities in response to stress.

senses.definition: The ability of a system to keep or return itself to a stable state without external help.
examples: The aircraft design aims for high autostability in turbulent air.
Autostability is an important property of feedback circuits.
The model demonstrates autostability over a wide range of temperatures.
